commit.message:
Support variadic generic value parameters. (#10415)

This PR adds support for variadic value parameters in generic
declarations, extending the existing variadic type parameter
infrastructure. The changes largely mirrors how variadics is handled for
type parameters.

Implementing language proposal [SP
#033](https://github.com/shader-slang/spec/pull/42).

### New syntax

```slang
// Modern syntax
struct Dims<let each D : int> {}

// Traditional syntax
struct Dims<each int D> {}

// Usage
Dims<1, 2, 3, 4> foo;
```

### What's included

**Core feature:**
- `GenericValuePackParamDecl` AST node for declaring value pack
parameters
- `ValuePackType(elementType)` AST type representing the type of a value
pack parameter
- `ConcreteIntValPack`, `ExpandIntValPack`, `EachIntVal` val nodes with
full substitution support
- `IRValuePackType(elementType)` IR type for generic value pack
parameters in IR generics
- Parser support for `let each D : int` and `each int D` syntax
- Generic argument matching groups trailing value arguments into packs
- `countof(D)` works on value packs (extended `SizeOfLikeIntVal` to
accept `Val*` instead of `Type*`)
- `expand`/`each` work on value packs: `expand printf("%d\n", each D)`,
`expand add(s, (each D) + 1)`
- Generic argument deduction from function arguments: `printDims(d)`
where `d : Dims<10,20,30>` infers `D = {10,20,30}`
- Forwarding and combining value packs: `Dims<A, expand (each B) + 1>`
- IR lowering produces `MakeValuePack` for concrete packs and
`IRValuePackType` for abstract pack parameters

**Generalized infrastructure (benefits both type packs and value
packs):**
- `ExpandType`'s captured packs widened from `Type*` to `Val*`, enabling
both type and value pack captures
- `m_capturedTypePacks` renamed to `m_capturedPacks`
(`OrderedHashSet<Val*>`)
- `isPackType()` helper checks for both type packs and value packs
- `isGenericParam()` and `isGenericValueParam()` utility functions
replace repetitive `as<>` checks
- `ReplaceScopeVisitor` extended with `ExpandExpr`, `EachExpr`,
`ParenExpr`, `TupleExpr`, `SharedTypeExpr`, `ModifiedTypeExpr`,
`PointerTypeExpr`, `FuncTypeExpr`, `TupleTypeExpr` (fixes scope
replacement for complex return type expressions)
- `ASTBuilder::getExpandIntValPack()` and `getEachIntVal()` with
identity folding (mirrors `getExpandType`/`getEachType`)

**Diagnostics:**
- Error when a non-pack generic parameter follows a pack parameter
- Note suggesting `let` keyword when `<D : int>` or `<each D : int>` is
used (only for inline constraints, not `where` clauses)

**Other:**
- Language server hover shows `(generic value pack parameter) let each D
: int`
- AST printing, name mangling, serialization support
- User guide documentation updated with variadic value parameter section

### Tests (19 test files)

| Test | Coverage |
|------|----------|
| `variadic-value-pack-0` | Basic struct + countof |
| `variadic-value-pack-empty` | Empty pack |
| `variadic-value-pack-expand-instantiate` | expand to instantiate
generic types |
| `variadic-value-pack-expand-sum` | expand to accumulate |
| `variadic-value-pack-forward-multiple` | Forward/combine packs, expand
arithmetic |
| `variadic-value-pack-generic-arg-deduce` | Inference from function
args |
| `variadic-value-pack-mixed-params` | Scalar + pack params |
| `variadic-value-pack-traditional-syntax` | `each int D` syntax |
| `variadic-value-pack-compute` | GPU compute shader |
| `variadic-value-pack-func-param` | Generic functions with packs |
| `variadic-value-pack-nested-generic` | Nested generic instantiation |
| `variadic-value-pack-nested-expand` | Complex arithmetic in expand |
| `variadic-value-pack-multi-capture` | expand capturing two packs |
| `variadic-value-pack-enum` | Enum element type |
| `variadic-value-pack-type-expr` | Pack params in return type with
expand/array |
| `variadic-value-pack-arg-count-mismatch` | Diagnostic: uneven arg
count |
| `generic-value-param-hint` | Diagnostic: let keyword hint |
| `generic-pack-param-position` | Diagnostic: pack not at end |
| `value-pack-param-hover` | Language server hover |

---------

projects simultaneously. Items are grouped by project in the tree
hierarchy and can be filtered by project at the runtime filter level.

Changes:
- Add editless.ado.projects array setting (name + enabled) with schema
- Deprecate editless.ado.project (singular) with backward-compat fallback
- Add project field to AdoWorkItem and AdoPR interfaces for origin tracking
- Fetch work items and PRs from all enabled projects in parallel
- Gracefully handle per-project fetch failures (don't break entire view)
- Update tree hierarchy: org → multiple project nodes (one per project)
- Add projects: string[] to WorkItemsFilter and PRsFilter interfaces
- Scope level-filter options and children rendering to specific projects
- Watch editless.ado.projects for config change debounce
- Update all tests for new interfaces (1198 tests pass)

Closes #487
Closes #496

docs: add human viewability guidance to image-returning tool docstrings (v0.0.6)

Add Human viewability note blocks to get_plate_thumbnail, get_plate_topview,
get_project_info (include_images path), and get_snapshot.

Rule: when the human user is the intended viewer (show me / open it / display
it / let me see it), call the browser-opening tool (open_plate_viewer,
open_plate_layout, view_stream) instead of returning a raw base64 data_uri.

Exception: when the AI agent itself is the consumer — describing or analyzing
the asset on the human's behalf, or processing raw bytes directly — the
raw-data tool is correct.

Remove dead “move block to new window” path and dependent unused APIs (#3002)

`WindowService.MoveBlockToNewWindow` appears to be unreferenced, and its
supporting path had become isolated. This change removes that dead RPC
surface and the backend/eventbus helpers that existed only for that
flow.

- **Window service cleanup (backend RPC)**
- Removed `MoveBlockToNewWindow_Meta` and `MoveBlockToNewWindow` from
`pkg/service/windowservice/windowservice.go`.
  - Dropped now-unused imports tied to that method (`log`, `eventbus`).

- **Store cleanup**
  - Removed `MoveBlockToTab` from `pkg/wstore/wstore.go`.
  - Removed now-unused `utilfn` import from the same file.

- **Eventbus cleanup**
  - Removed unused event constant `WSEvent_ElectronNewWindow`.
- Removed `getWindowWatchesForWindowId` and `BusyWaitForWindowId`, which
were only used by the deleted move-to-new-window path.
  - Removed now-unused `time` import.

- **Generated frontend service surface**
- Regenerated service bindings and removed
`WindowServiceType.MoveBlockToNewWindow(...)` from
`frontend/app/store/services.ts`.

Example of removed RPC surface:
```ts
// removed from frontend/app/store/services.ts
MoveBlockToNewWindow(currentTabId: string, blockId: string): Promise<void> {
    return WOS.callBackendService("window", "MoveBlockToNewWindow", Array.from(arguments))
}
```

commit.message:
fix: fall back to globalType.defaultDayRate in resource profile cost calculation

Project-level resource types inherit day rates from global types, but the
resource profile query only fetched resourceTypes: true (no globalType join).
When dayRate was null on the project ResourceType, hasCost stayed false and
cost columns were hidden even though global rates were set.

Fix: include globalType in both resourceTypes and overheads queries, and
resolve: resourceType.dayRate ?? resourceType.globalType?.defaultDayRate

Fix autocomplete: use fetch ID instead of query comparison for stale guard

The previous lastQuery check was too aggressive — every keystroke updated
lastQuery, causing debounced responses to be discarded as 'stale' even
when they were the most recent fetch. This made autocomplete appear to
wait until typing stopped, and Nominatim results (Barclays Center, etc.)
were silently dropped.

Now uses an incrementing fetchId that only changes when a new fetch
actually fires after debounce, so results are shown promptly.

commit.message:
Wrapper: generate Base.getproperty accessors for byte-blob structs

Generate property accessors that use DWARF member offsets to provide
named field access on byte-blob structs. For primitive members, uses
unsafe_load with typed pointers. For nested struct members, checks
whether the target is a byte-blob (extract bytes) or normal struct
(unsafe_load typed pointer). Handles embedded-vs-standalone size
mismatches with zero-padding.

Also adds test/realworld_eigen/verify.jl integration test covering
all 5 API functions with struct size and property accessor assertions.
